pipeline.heading: Build your Agent or a complete AI Application in 3 steps.
pipeline.subheading: Start with a prompt and a connection to your data. We'll handle the rest.

steps.title: Gather Data Dictionary
steps.desc: Align on core data schemas, metrics, and business terminology to establish a unified foundation.

steps.title: Integrate Medallion Architecture
steps.desc: Organize data into Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers to ensure quality, reliability, and performance.

steps.title: Configure Agent Parameters
steps.desc: Set the initial rules, boundaries, and reasoning paths for your custom AI agent.

steps.title: Test & Iterate Business Guidelines
steps.desc: Validate agent responses against established business rules to ensure strict compliance and accuracy.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.desc: Launch your custom AI application with full observability, ready to answer questions and fetch data.

features.heading: Why Businesses Love Agent Factory
features.subheading: It works the way they think. No SQL. No tickets to the data team. Just plain-English questions and answers they can act on.

items.title: One Agent per Function. Each one an Expert.
items.description: Agent Factory doesn't throw one generic AI Agent at every business question. It deploys specialist agents trained on each function's KPIs, vocabulary, and decision patterns.

items.title: Answers that Explain Themselves.
items.description: Every answer shows the reasoning behind it. What was queried, what logic was applied, why the number looks the way it does. Your team sees the work, not just the output.

items.title: Charts, Breakdowns, and Trend Lines on Demand.
items.description: Agent Factory shows you the insight in every format that makes decision making easier. Ask "how did revenue trend last quarter" and get a chart. Ask "break it down by region" and the view updates.

items.title: Agents that Get Smarter Every Week
items.description: Most AI tools stay stagnate. Agent Factory can be continuously tuned with new KPIs, changing business rules, evolving data models so the agents stay relevant as your business changes.

faq.heading: Everything you need to know about Agent Factory.
faq.subheading: Architecture, deployment, timelines — the questions we get on every first call before getting started.

items.question: Is Agent Factory a SaaS product?
items.answer: No. It's a productized service. Our engineers deploy and configure it on your data stack, tuned to your business. You get the speed of a product with the precision of a custom build.

items.question: What data stack does it work with?
items.answer: Databricks, Snowflake, or any cloud data warehouse you already run. Agent Factory sits on top of your existing infrastructure — nothing to migrate, nothing to replace.

items.question: How long does it take to go live?
items.answer: 2 to 4 weeks from kickoff. Week one is discovery — mapping your data, KPIs, and business questions. By week four, your first agents are live in production.

items.question: Who uses it day to day?
items.answer: Business leaders — CMOs, CFOs, VPs of Sales, operations heads. Not your data team. The whole point is that the people making decisions can get to the data themselves, without writing SQL or opening a dashboard.

items.question: How is this different from a BI tool?
items.answer: BI tools give you dashboards. Agent Factory gives you answers. You ask a question in plain English, and a domain-tuned agent responds with the insight — a chart, a breakdown, a recommendation. No filters, no drag-and-drop, no waiting.

items.question: What about data security?
items.answer: Your data stays in your warehouse. Agent Factory doesn't move it, copy it, or store it. Your existing governance and access controls stay exactly as they are.

items.question: What happens after go-live?
items.answer: Our engineering team continuously tunes your agents — new KPIs, updated business rules, changing data models. You don't maintain it. We do.

items.question: How is this different from ChatGPT or a generic AI assistant?
items.answer: Generic AI tools don't know your data, your KPIs, or your business context. Agent Factory deploys domain-specific agents trained on your actual data stack. The difference is the same as asking a stranger for advice versus asking an analyst who's worked at your company for two years.

cta.heading: It's not 2016 anymore.
cta.subheading: You shouldn't need a week, a dashboard, or a data team to make one decision.

useCases.heading: Stale Insights will Never Give You Margin
useCases.subheading: McKinsey says, businesses that take fast data-driven decisions are 19x more likely to be profitable.

items.title: A Global F&B Company's Recipe for Profit in the Age of AI
items.description: "A global F&B conglomerate was over-discounting without realizing it. AI surfaced this insight in real time. They fixed their pricing strategy and revenue jumped 5-10%."

items.title: A Supply Chain Dept. that Got Faster with AI
items.description: “The Supply Chain department of a CPG company was struggling to keep up with demand fluctuations due to slow data insights. AI gave them real-time visibility into their supply chain. Forecasting errors dropped by 30%”

items.title: When AI Grows Your Bottom-Line
items.description: "A leading consulting firm found that CPG companies applying AI for real-time insights across commercial functions unlock 3–5 percentage points of EBITDA growth."

product.heading: But, Why are Business Slow in Decision Making?
product.subheading: Millions of dollars are spent every year on data initiatives, yet insights take more than a week to arrive.

items.title: Stakeholders Wait Up to 2 Weeks for Data Insights.
items.description: Heavy reliance on analytics teams creates bottlenecks, slowing down access to the data needed for timely and informed decision-making.

items.title: Data Analysts Stuck in a Sea of Ad-Hoc Requests
items.description: Flooded with low-impact requests, analysts spend their time handling routine tasks, leaving little room for high-value strategic analysis.

items.title: Just Dashboards aren't Enough for Actionable Insights
items.description: Most dashboards are static, offering only BAU metrics for health checks. They rarely empower stakeholders to extract meaningful insights for strategic decision-making.
